explanation: very simple exterior ambiences. The exterior equivalent of a room tone. If noticable activity find a better fitting category.

discriminators: UCS: 'quiet exterior air with little activity ... if noticeable activity find a better fitting category.' So the discriminator against every other AMBIENCE is near-zero onset rate and near-zero spectral flux, not timbre.
Exterior counterpart of AMBIENCE-ROOM TONE. DSP cannot tell exterior air from interior room tone; ROOM TONE tends narrower stereo and more low-frequency HVAC energy, but that is a nudge, not a proof.
Any file with birds, insects, traffic or voices over the bed belongs elsewhere, and its flux will say so.

explanation: for recordings and ambiences geographically linked to Alpine Environments. Some overlap with AMBIENCE-RURAL.

discriminators: Mountain ambience: a quiet wind-and-distance bed. UCS itself notes 'some overlap with AMBIENCE-RURAL'.

DSP cannot hear altitude. There is no measurement that separates an alpine meadow from a rural meadow or a grassland -- only the filename can. The gates here exist to rule out one-shots, nothing more.

explanation: Theme parks and carnivals. Beware of possible rights issues with uncleared music. Carnivals

discriminators: Theme parks, carnivals, fairs: a crowd bed plus rides, PA and often music.
Versus AMBIENCE-CELEBRATION and AMBIENCE-MARKET: acoustically the same populated-crowd texture. The distinction is what the place is called, not what it measures.
Music content raises harmonicity and can add a non-zero beats_per_minute, but that is a hint, not a rule.

explanation: like a dawn chorus of pretty birds
where the birds are the prominent sound. Single birds would go in BIRDS-SONGBIRD.

discriminators: UCS: 'group songbirds ... where the birds are the prominent sound'. Dense whistled chirps in 2-8 kHz: high-frequency dominant like AMBIENCE-INSECT, but eventful rather than stationary.
Versus AMBIENCE-INSECT: birdsong has discrete onsets, real harmonicity (whistled tones) and much higher spectral flux; an insect bed is a flat continuous shimmer with near-zero flux.
UCS: 'single birds would go in BIRDS-SONGBIRD' -- a one-shot call fails the ambience length gate.

explanation: New Years Eve. Not pure crowds
as that would go in CROWDS
but i.e. exterior city new years. Also see AMBIENCES-AMUSEMENT for something like a fair or festival.

discriminators: UCS: parties, New Year's Eve -- 'not pure crowds, as that would go in CROWDS', i.e. a crowd bed embedded in a wider scene (exterior city, fireworks, music).

Versus CROWDS-CELEBRATION: identical voicing signature. The difference is scene versus isolated element, which shows up only as a slightly wider stereo image and more non-vocal content -- not decisive.
Versus AMBIENCE-AMUSEMENT: UCS routes fairs and festivals there.

explanation: Construction scenes with things like jackhammers

discriminators: Composite construction scene: jackhammers, cranes, pile drivers. Loud, machine-dominated, high crest from repeated heavy impacts over a diesel bed.

Versus AMBIENCE-INDUSTRIAL: both are machine beds and DSP cannot tell a construction site from a factory floor. Construction leans more impulsive (impacts, hammering); industrial leans more stationary. A tendency, not a test.
Isolated machines belong in MACHINES-CONSTRUCTION or VEHICLES-CONSTRUCTION; the length gate rules those out.

explanation: Quiet desert ambiences with appropriate insects and birds. Maybe tumbleweeds.

discriminators: UCS: 'quiet desert ambiences with appropriate insects and birds'. A sparse, quiet, wide bed -- often little more than wind with occasional insect activity.

DSP cannot distinguish desert from tundra, alpine or plain exterior AIR. All are quiet, stationary, low-flux beds. If insects dominate the file it should be AMBIENCE-INSECT, and the high-band energy will say so.

explanation: Artificial designed ambience. V8.2 adds AMBIENCE-FANTASY and AMBIENCE-SCIFI which may be better fit for most 'designed' categories.

discriminators: A provenance distinction, not an acoustic one: 'artificial designed ambience' means it was built in a DAW, not that it sounds like anything in particular. A designed forest and a recorded forest measure the same.
UCS steers most designed beds to AMBIENCE-FANTASY or AMBIENCE-SCIFI; this is the residue. Signal scoring is disabled -- filename and metadata decide.

explanation: ambulances and medics. Also see VEHICLES-EMERGENCY.

discriminators: Crime, fire and accident scenes: a scene bed with sirens, radios, voices and engines.
Sirens are the one measurable cue -- strong harmonicity with a periodic pitch sweep -- but a siren in a file does not prove an emergency scene, and VEHICLES-SIREN is where isolated sirens belong.
Versus AMBIENCE-URBAN: an emergency scene is a city bed with a specific event on top. Semantic.

explanation: Magical or other wordly composite ambiences

discriminators: A designed otherworldly soundscape: layered drones, processed nature, wide stereo, dense low end.
Versus AMBIENCE-SCIFI: fantasy leans organic/reverberant, scifi leans mechanical/tonal -- but both are authored, and no measurement decides Middle Earth versus a starship bridge.
Versus AMBIENCE-DESIGNED: a pure provenance sibling; UCS says to prefer FANTASY or SCIFI where they fit.

explanation: etc. Isolated farm animals would go into ANIMALS-FARM.

discriminators: Farm scene: animals, tractors, chickens, open-air bed.
UCS: 'isolated farm animals would go into ANIMALS-FARM' -- a single cow is a one-shot and fails the length gate.
Versus AMBIENCE-RURAL and AMBIENCE-GRASSLAND: UCS routes anything with farm animals or human activity here, but no DSP feature detects livestock. Semantic.

explanation: Forest recordings or forest composite files. Trees and birds
maybe wind in trees all together. Some overlap with AMBIENCE-RURAL or AMBIENCE-ALPINE.

discriminators: Trees, birds and wind-in-leaves together. Wide, stationary, mid-to-high-frequency bed with sparse bird onsets.
UCS notes overlap with AMBIENCE-RURAL and AMBIENCE-ALPINE, and no measurement separates them.
If birds are the prominent sound the file is AMBIENCE-BIRDSONG (much higher high-band energy and flux); if wind in the trees is the whole file it is WIND-VEGETATION.

explanation: etc. But FARMS has own category if farm animals or human activity are present.

discriminators: Prairies, meadows, grazing pasture: an open exterior bed, wind through grass, insects, distant birds.
UCS: 'FARMS has own category if farm animals or human activity are present' -- that is the only stated rule, and it is a semantic one.
Indistinguishable by DSP from AMBIENCE-RURAL, AMBIENCE-ALPINE or a quiet AMBIENCE-DESERT.

explanation: Roman street scenes. Composite history and ancient ambiences.

discriminators: Old West streets, Roman crowds: a period scene, usually a populated exterior bed with horses, carts and walla.
This is a costume label. Acoustically it is AMBIENCE-TOWN or AMBIENCE-MARKET with no modern traffic; absence of engine rumble is a weak hint at best, and a quiet modern village measures the same.
Text is the only reliable evidence.

explanation: control rooms. James Bond type lairs. Spaceship interiors.

discriminators: Data centres, control rooms, spaceship interiors: a stationary fan/HVAC bed, often with sparse beeps.
Physically this is a loud room tone. Versus AMBIENCE-ROOM TONE it is louder and broader-band, but there is no threshold that reliably separates a server room from a noisy office.
Versus AMBIENCE-SCIFI: SCIFI is a composite designed scene; HITECH is the machine bed under it.

explanation: surgery and emergency rooms too. Possibly veterinarian hospitals too.

discriminators: Hospital interiors: a quiet interior bed with sparse events -- monitors, footsteps, distant voices, doors.

This is the canonical semantic_only case. AMBIENCE-HOSPITAL, AMBIENCE-SCHOOL and AMBIENCE-OFFICE are the same measurement: quiet reverberant interior, low flux, sparse onsets, some walla. Any numeric distinction between them would be fabricated.
Only the filename, or a learned embedding cued on 'monitor', 'ICU', 'ward', can decide.

explanation: plants or any ambience with an industrial feeling.

discriminators: Factories, plants, warehouses: a loud, stationary machine bed with strong low-frequency energy and often periodic mechanical rhythm.
Versus AMBIENCE-CONSTRUCTION: same machine physics. Industrial is steadier, construction is more impulsive -- a tendency only.
Versus MACHINES-INDUSTRIAL: that is an isolated machine, this is a room full of them; only length and stereo width hint at the difference.

explanation: cicada and cricket beds mainly. May contain over sounds but main sound being large presence of insects. See also ANIMALS-INSECT for more isolated sounds.

discriminators: A cicada/cricket bed is the one truly measurable nature ambience: dense narrowband energy in 2-8 kHz, almost no energy below 200 Hz, and an extremely stationary texture.
Versus AMBIENCE-BIRDSONG: insects are far more stationary and far less tonal. Birdsong has higher spectral flux, discrete sparse onsets and real harmonicity; an insect bed is a continuous shimmer.
UCS: 'more isolated sounds' go to ANIMALS-INSECT -- a single chirp is a one-shot, not a bed, and the length gate rules it out.

explanation: but the lake version of AMBIENCE-SEASIDE. See also WATER-LAPPING.

discriminators: Lakeside scene: lapping water plus the surrounding environment (birds, boats, distant people).
UCS: 'not isolated lapping' -- a clean lapping recording is WATER-LAP.
Versus AMBIENCE-SEASIDE: a lake has no surf. Absence of the broadband low-frequency surf wash is a genuine physical difference, but it is unreliable on a windy lake, so this stays semantic.

explanation: Busy exterior or interior market activity and crowds. Think vibrant international markets in Istanbul or Africa

discriminators: Busy market: dense human walla with vendor calls, handling and transactions over it.

Strong voicing ratio and 3-8 Hz syllabic modulation distinguish this from any non-vocal ambience, but not from AMBIENCE-RESTAURANT & BAR, AMBIENCE-PUBLIC PLACE or CROWDS-WALLA, which measure identically.
Text is what decides between a bazaar and a hotel lobby.

explanation: Ambience sounds not fitting another category in this list.

discriminators: The abstain target for AMBIENCE: 'ambience sounds not fitting another category in this list.'
It carries only the shared ambience gates -- a bed, not a one-shot -- and deliberately weak priors, so that it wins by default when no sibling clears threshold and loses whenever one does.

explanation: things revolving around ships. (compare to AMBIENCE-SEASIDE). Water only recordings would go to a WATER category.

discriminators: Ships, harbours and shipyards: hull creaks, engines, rigging, gulls, water against steel.
UCS: 'water only recordings would go to a WATER category' -- a clean surf or lapping bed is WATER, not this.
Versus AMBIENCE-SEASIDE: SEASIDE is a beach scene with people, NAUTICAL is a scene about vessels. No DSP feature separates them; both are wide low-frequency water-and-wind beds.

explanation: telephones. General offices of various sizes.

discriminators: Workplace interior: a quiet HVAC bed with typing, phones and low conversation.
Indistinguishable by DSP from AMBIENCE-SCHOOL or AMBIENCE-HOSPITAL -- all three are 'quiet interior bed with sparse events'. Inventing a numeric boundary between them would be dishonest.
Versus AMBIENCE-ROOM TONE: an office has measurable activity (non-zero onset rate and flux); a room tone does not. That boundary IS real.

explanation: probably usually with some human activity
city activity. Not pure nature sounds.

discriminators: City or suburban park: nature bed with human activity over it. UCS: 'not pure nature sounds.'

The presence of voices and distant traffic is what separates a park from AMBIENCE-FOREST or AMBIENCE-GRASSLAND -- measurable in principle (voicing ratio, low-frequency traffic wash), unreliable in practice.
Versus AMBIENCE-SUBURBAN: same bed with more or less pavement. Semantic.
Prisons and jails: reverberant inmate walla, jail doors, buzzers.

The only measurable trait UCS names is reverberance -- long decay on a hard-surfaced interior -- which shows up as a long decay_time_seconds_60db. It does not separate a prison from a school gymnasium.
Sits with HOSPITAL, SCHOOL and OFFICE as an interior scene decided by text.

explanation: Riots. Big mixed ambiences of crowds

discriminators: Protests, rallies, riots: shouting, yelling and chanting crowds.

UCS: 'opposed to AMBIENCE-CHEERING'. Chanting is the one real signal cue -- a strong onset periodicity in the 0.5-2 Hz range that an applauding or cheering crowd does not have -- but it is present only intermittently.
Versus CROWDS-ANGRY: same voicing, isolated element versus full scene.

explanation: for any other usually indoor public places such as stores
museums. Lots of possible overlap with other categories. Use if not fitting elsewhere.

discriminators: Indoor public places -- stores, lobbies, hotels, malls, museums. A reverberant interior bed with diffuse walla.
UCS itself calls it the interior catch-all: 'lots of possible overlap with other categories. Use if not fitting elsewhere.'
It measures like AMBIENCE-SCHOOL, AMBIENCE-HOSPITAL and AMBIENCE-MARKET. It should never be asserted from signal alone.

explanation: pilgrimages. Any ambience relating to religion.

discriminators: Churches, temples, services: a very reverberant interior with organ, chant, bells or murmured prayer.
Long reverb decay and, when singing or chanting is present, high harmonicity are genuine cues -- but they point at 'large resonant hall', not at 'religious', and a concert hall measures the same.
Versus CROWDS-SINGING: isolated element versus full scene.

explanation: Something busier than a room tone
a House with some activity from outside etc. Interior with exterior sounds coming through closed or open doors

discriminators: UCS: 'something busier than a room tone' -- an apartment or house with exterior sound bleeding in through doors and windows.
The boundary against AMBIENCE-ROOM TONE is the one measurable thing here: RESIDENTIAL has non-zero onset rate, flux and often low-frequency traffic bleed; ROOM TONE has none.
Against AMBIENCE-OFFICE, AMBIENCE-SCHOOL and the other interiors: semantic.

explanation: mixed crowd with glass clinks or silverware.

discriminators: Restaurants, bars and pubs: mixed crowd walla with glass and silverware clinks over it.
The clinks are real -- sparse bright high-crest transients on a voiced bed -- and are the only feature that leans this way rather than to a generic walla, but they are far too sparse to gate on.
Otherwise identical in measurement to AMBIENCE-PUBLIC PLACE, AMBIENCE-MARKET and CROWDS-WALLA.

explanation: Simple room tones with little or no activity. Also see now AMBIENCE-RESIDENTIAL if more than just air is present
as it might be a better fit.

discriminators: UCS: 'simple room tones with little or no activity'. The signature is near-silence with a stationary low-frequency HVAC/air bed: flux and onset rate essentially zero.
Louder or eventful interiors are AMBIENCE-RESIDENTIAL ('something busier than a room tone'). The boundary is measurable: any appreciable onset rate or flux disqualifies ROOM TONE.
Versus AMBIENCE-AIR: same physics, interior instead of exterior. Narrower stereo image and more sub-500 Hz energy lean this way, but the call is semantic.

explanation: Rural and countryside ambiences that don't fit somewhere else in this list. Nature

discriminators: Countryside away from signs of people. UCS explicitly lists it as the overflow for ALPINE, DESERT, FOREST and GRASSLAND, and says so: 'ambiences that don't fit somewhere else in this list.'
Being the residual of a semantic family, it is itself semantic. Its gates rule out one-shots; nothing more.
If human or traffic content is prominent the file belongs in TOWN or SUBURBAN, and voicing ratio will hint at it.
Schools: hallways, lockers, voices, bells.
Acoustically identical to AMBIENCE-OFFICE and AMBIENCE-HOSPITAL -- a reverberant interior bed with sparse events and intermittent walla. Any precise numeric split between them would be invented.
School bells are a strong tonal transient, but they are also just BELLS, and a school recording usually has none.

explanation: a complex background layered with many sci-fi elements. A complex spacehip bridge
or high-tech lab. See also SCIFI for individual elements.

discriminators: A composite, authored sci-fi bed: layered drones, hums, beeps and mechanisms -- 'a complex spaceship bridge or high-tech lab.'
Versus AMBIENCE-HITECH: HITECH is a real machine-room recording, SCIFI is a designed composite. Physically similar; the difference is provenance and layering.
UCS: 'see also SCIFI for individual elements' -- an isolated beep or door is SCIFI-*, not this.

explanation: Relating to beach and oceanside scenes with people. (Compare to AMBIENCE-NAUTICAL). Water only recordings would go to a WATER category.

discriminators: Beach and oceanside scenes with people: a broadband surf wash plus gulls, wind and voices.
UCS: 'water only recordings would go to a WATER category' -- a clean surf bed is WATER-SURF. Presence of voicing is what makes it SEASIDE, and that is measurable but weak.
Versus AMBIENCE-NAUTICAL: shore scene with people versus vessel scene. Semantic. Versus AMBIENCE-LAKESIDE: surf versus lapping -- physically real, but easily confounded by wind.

explanation: little league baseball to major league. Use CROWDS-SPORT for clean crowd recordings.

discriminators: Sporting events: a large crowd bed with PA, whistles and periodic cheer swells.
UCS: 'use CROWDS-SPORT for clean crowd recordings' -- this is the scene, that is the element, and they measure the same.
Versus AMBIENCE-PROTEST and AMBIENCE-CELEBRATION: all three are large voiced crowds. Only text separates them.
Suburban street: UCS says 'not dense like URBAN' -- lawnmowers, sprinklers, birds, occasional single car by.

The URBAN / TOWN / SUBURBAN triple is a density continuum. Low-frequency traffic energy does fall monotonically along it, but the boundaries are arbitrary and UCS itself warns of confusion. Setting a numeric threshold between a town and a suburb would be a lie dressed as math.
Bird content and sparse discrete pass-bys lean suburban; a continuous traffic wash leans urban.

explanation: A wetland often partially or intermittently covered with water; especially : one dominated by woody vegetation. Marshes

discriminators: Wetlands: frogs, insects, birds and water. Usually a dense, stationary, high-frequency-rich biophony.
Versus AMBIENCE-INSECT: if insects dominate, that category wins on high-band energy. Frogs add mid-band croaking with a distinctive slow onset rhythm, but no feature in the vocabulary isolates them.
Versus AMBIENCE-TROPICAL and AMBIENCE-LAKESIDE: same biophonic bed. Semantic.

explanation: Something in between a CITY and a SUBURB. Activity
that 'small town' or village sound. May be confusion between this

discriminators: UCS: 'something in between a CITY and a SUBURB ... may be confusion between this, AMBIENCE-URBAN and AMBIENCE-SUBURBAN.' The standard is written as an admission of ambiguity.
Traffic wash plus pedestrian walla, at a middling density. There is no honest numeric cut against URBAN on one side or SUBURBAN on the other.
Filename is the evidence.

explanation: Specifically traffic without people. Not for a single car by
clean highway traffic for example. Rush hour where the sound is predominantly cars.

discriminators: A busy-traffic wash is measurable: a stationary low-frequency rumble bed, centroid well under 1 kHz, very little above 2 kHz.
UCS: 'specifically traffic without people' -- appreciable voicing or syllabic modulation pushes the file to AMBIENCE-URBAN or AMBIENCE-TOWN, which are the same rumble plus voices.
UCS: 'not for a single car by' -- a single pass-by is a sweep with a Doppler centroid slope, not a stationary bed.

explanation: subway stations. Not the airplane or train itself. But public places centering around transportation.

discriminators: Stations, airports, terminals -- 'not the airplane or train itself. But public places centering around transportation.' A large reverberant concourse: diffuse walla, PA announcements, rolling luggage, distant engines.
PA speech has a band-limited, futzed quality, which is a real cue, but the same bed appears in AMBIENCE-PUBLIC PLACE.
An isolated train or aircraft is TRAINS-* or AIRCRAFT-*, and would be far shorter and far more eventful.
Jungle and rainforest: dense, layered, high-frequency biophony -- insects, birds, frogs, all at once.

It is the densest of the nature beds: very high stationarity with very high high-band energy. That does separate it from a temperate AMBIENCE-FOREST in the average case, but the overlap is total in the tails.
Versus AMBIENCE-INSECT: if cicadas alone dominate, that is INSECT. Versus AMBIENCE-SWAMP: semantic.

explanation: Treeless environments where the ground is permanently frozen

discriminators: Arctic and antarctic: treeless, near-lifeless. Acoustically this is almost always wind over emptiness -- a quiet, stationary, low-flux bed with essentially no biophony.
That absence is measurable (near-zero high-band chirp energy, near-zero onset rate) but it is exactly the same measurement as AMBIENCE-AIR or a still AMBIENCE-DESERT.
No DSP feature encodes temperature.
Sewers, bunkers, tunnels, caves: a heavily reverberant, low-frequency-weighted interior with sparse drips and long decays.
Long reverb decay and a dark spectrum are genuine cues, but they describe 'large hard enclosed space', which also fits a cathedral or an empty warehouse.
Versus AMBIENCE-UNDERWATER: that one is low-passed by water, not by reverb -- its high-band energy collapses, while a tunnel keeps its drip transients bright.

explanation: deep bubbles and that classic undersea world sound. See also WATER-UNDERWATER.

discriminators: The only AMBIENCE with a hard physical signature: water is a strong low-pass. Steep spectral slope, low band-limit, centroid a few hundred Hz, near-nothing above 2 kHz.
Versus WATER-UNDERWATER: same filtering; that one is an isolated water event, this is a full undersea bed ('deep bubbles and that classic undersea world sound') and runs long.
The high-band ceiling is set generously so a snapping-shrimp reef is not wrongly gated out.

explanation: Dense cities and environments. New York City or London or Mumbai. Often Dense Traffic
but really any city. Overlap with AMBIENCE-TOWN possible.

discriminators: Dense city: continuous traffic wash, horns, pedestrians, buses. UCS: 'overlap with AMBIENCE-TOWN possible.'
It is AMBIENCE-TRAFFIC plus voices: strong low-frequency rumble AND appreciable voicing. Traffic alone, with no people, is AMBIENCE-TRAFFIC by explicit UCS rule -- that boundary is measurable.
Against TOWN and SUBURBAN it is only a matter of density, and no honest threshold exists.

explanation: full ambiences here. Not for isolated WEAPONS or VEHICLES which have their own categories. Composite sounds of war.

discriminators: Battle ambience: a composite of gunfire, explosions, vehicles and shouting. High kurtosis -- isolated violent bursts over a bed -- and very high dynamic range.
UCS: 'not for isolated WEAPONS or VEHICLES which have their own categories.' Those are one-shots and fail the length gate; this is a scene.
Versus CROWDS-BATTLE: that is the crowd element alone. Versus AMBIENCE-EMERGENCY: both are chaotic scene beds.
